home.title: Kandt House Museum: Interactive Heritage for Digital-Native Visitors
home.subtitle: Today's young visitors grow up swiping screens, watching videos, and interacting with digital content daily; yet when they walk into a museum, they are often handed a printed brochure and a static label. We bridge that disconnect with a QR-enabled AR Smart Tourism System for the Kandt House Museum.
home.webArDesc: Scan QR codes on site to instantly open immersive content, 3D reconstructions, and narrated stories.
home.accessibleDesc: Built as a Web-AR solution, the experience runs directly in the browser with no app download needed.
home.digitalDesc: The system turns on-site QR codes into interactive storytelling experiences for young tourists and museum staff.
home.exploreSubtitle: Everything you need for your visit, all in one place

home.researchDesc: Developed using Design Science Research and mixed-method evaluation, this solution is practical, affordable, and scalable.

trail.title: Richard Kandt Trail
trail.intro: Richard Kandt (1867–1918) — German explorer and physician. He was the first European to explore the Nile-Congo divide and later became the first colonial resident of Rwanda. Follow the timeline below to trace his journey and legacy.
trail.noEvents: No trail events have been added yet.

home.aboutText: The Kandt House Museum, located in Kigali, Rwanda, is housed in the former residence of Dr. Richard Kandt — the first German colonial governor of Rwanda. Today it serves as a natural history museum showcasing Rwanda's rich biodiversity, geological heritage, and the story of Kigali's founding. The museum preserves collections of fauna, flora, minerals, and historical artifacts that tell the story of Rwanda's natural and cultural evolution.
home.historyText: Built in the early 1900s, the house stands as one of Kigali's oldest colonial-era structures. After independence, it was repurposed as a space for preserving Rwanda's natural heritage. The museum now features interactive AR-enabled exhibitions that bring history to life for a new generation of visitors.
home.visitHours: Mon–Sat: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sun: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM
home.visitAdmission: Admission: Free for students, 3,000 RWF for adults
